message:
feat(core,abi): port /proc/self/maps line parser to core::proc_maps (bd-pm-epic)

Two abi files duplicated /proc/self/maps line parsing:
host_resolve.rs::parse_maps_line (~25 lines, full parse with libc.so +
perms + offset filters) and pthread_abi.rs::parse_maps_range (~7
lines, just the start-end address pair). Lift both into a single
pure-safe core::proc_maps module.

New core surface:
  pub struct MapsEntry<'a> { start, end, perms, offset, dev, inode, path }
  pub fn parse_maps_line(line: &str) -> Option<MapsEntry<'_>>
  pub fn parse_maps_range(line: &str) -> Option<(usize, usize)>

The full parser handles: typical mapped-library lines with paths,
anonymous mappings (no path), pseudo-named regions ([stack], [heap],
[vdso], [vvar]), high 64-bit kernel addresses (0xffff_ffff_8000_0000),
all permission-string variants (r--p, rw-p, r-xp, rwxp, ---p, r--s,
rw-s), EOL stripping (\n, \r\n), and rejecting truncated/malformed/
extra-token lines.

abi changes:
  - host_resolve.rs::parse_maps_line shrinks ~25 -> ~16 lines: it
    now applies the libc.so + r--p + offset==0 filter on the
    MapsEntry returned by core, then packs the path into a
    [u8; 512] for the caller.
  - pthread_abi.rs::parse_maps_range becomes a 1-line forwarder
    to core::proc_maps::parse_maps_range.

Verified:
  - 20 new core::proc_maps unit tests pass
  - 173/173 abi lib tests still pass

Closes bd-pm-epic.
